crime.value: Blood poisoning murders - Marie Leroux (La Constantin) and Claude Perregaud poisoning victims
crime.quote: the two deaths by blood-poisoning of Angelique de Querchi and Charlotte Boullenois

victim.value: Angelique de Querchi and Charlotte Boullenois - targets of an infamous criminal enterprise
victim.quote: the two deaths by blood-poisoning of Angelique de Querchi and Charlotte Boullenois

closed_world.value: Paris criminal underworld centered around tavern in rue Saint-Andre-des-Arts and associated locations
closed_world.quote: introduce the reader to a little tavern in Paris, situated in the rue Saint-Andre-des-Arts

culprit_and_motive.value: Marie Leroux (widow Constantin) and Claude Perregaud operating an infamous criminal calling for profit
culprit_and_motive.quote: they had amassed immense fortunes in the exercise of their infamous calling

resolution.value: Parliamentary decree condemning criminals to death after exhumation proved guilt and revealed their operation
resolution.quote: They were condemned by parliamentary decree to be hanged by the neck till they were dead

investigator.value: Quennebert (the pretended chevalier) who exposed the crimes and provided key evidence
investigator.quote: He produced the declarations made by Mademoiselle de Guerchi and the commander, and had the body exhumed

suspect_architecture.value: Multiple suspects with overlapping romantic/financial motives: Marie Leroux (widow Constantin), Claude Perregaud (accomplice), Chevalier de Moranges, Duc de Vitry, Commander, Treasurer - all connected through Angelique and criminal enterprise
suspect_architecture.quote: Three gentlemen... grew pale to the lips, and stood staring at the door with outstretched arms

red_herring.value: The duel between Chevalier and Duc de Vitry serves as misdirection from the real criminal enterprise of poisoning operated by La Constantin and Perregaud
red_herring.quote: Both drew at the same moment. Two shrieks followed, one in the room, the other behind the tapestry

clue_fairness.value: Book entries and exhumation evidence are presented to reader - sparse book records would reveal scandals in high places, body examination proves poisoning claims
clue_fairness.quote: The entries in the books seized at their house, though sparse, would have led... to scandals

social_world.value: Post-Fronde French aristocracy with deep corruption across all classes, involving high-placed individuals whose scandals must be concealed to protect social hierarchy
social_world.quote: the low morals and deep corruption into which all classes of society had sunk at the termination

alibi.value: Quennebert's legal defense involves producing declarations from Mademoiselle de Guerchi and the commander to establish his innocence in Charlotte's death
alibi.quote: He produced the declarations made by Mademoiselle de Guerchi and the commander

reveal_mechanic.value: Exhumation of the body provides physical proof that validates the seemingly extraordinary defense claims about blood-poisoning rather than natural death
reveal_mechanic.quote: the exhumation proved the truth of his assertions... deaths by blood-poisoning

media_and_audience.value: Narrator explicitly addresses reader about withholding revolting details, shaping public consumption of crime narrative while protecting social sensibilities
media_and_audience.quote: we must warn the reader... the healthy-minded reader will be grateful for our reticence

_meta.title: ebook alexandre dumas pere la constantin
